key_insights.insight: Acceptance of what you cannot change is liberating and essential for moving forward.
key_insights.quote: Acceptance actually can be a beautiful thing and it can be a liberating thing to think, I'm not holding on to something that I can't change.

key_insights.insight: When making a big decision, waiting for 100% certainty can be more damaging than acting with 51% certainty.
key_insights.quote: What ends up happening is the procrastination of the decision ends up costing you more in the long term than actually just making the decision and finding out if you're right or wrong.

key_insights.insight: True freedom comes from prioritizing 'no' over 'yes' by focusing on what truly resonates with you.
key_insights.quote: It's now I'm so, it's the no's that are more important to me than the yes's.

key_insights.insight: You shouldn't focus on turning your weaknesses into strengths; instead, double down on your strengths.
key_insights.quote: Don't try and turn your D in physics to a C in physics. Just focus on the A.

key_insights.insight: Starting a business is all-consuming and you have to be aware of how much control is needed to be a successful owner.
key_insights.quote: If you're going to start a business, you have to know that don't think you're going to turn your phone off at five because that's not a thing. It's not a thing. And if you turn your phone off at five, don't expect a successful business.

key_insights.insight: It's essential to separate past traumas from current relationships to foster growth.
key_insights.quote: I don't think you can ever let any relationship grow at its full potential if you're still dealing with old shit.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes not reacting and letting the noise die down is the best approach when facing criticism.
key_insights.quote: You don't explain and you don't complain.

key_insights.insight: Do something that you can deliver and will work your ass off at.
key_insights.quote: Let me do something that I know that I can deliverand I'll work my ass off at that.

actionable_advice.advice: Interview yourself before committing to a project or brand.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pretend you are being interviewed on television, answering questions about why you are involved and gauge if you can answer with genuine conviction.

actionable_advice.advice: When facing online criticism, resist the urge to immediately defend yourself.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take a step back, assess if the situation calls for a response, and prioritize the bigger picture, protect yourself from emotional reactivity by setting boundaries.

actionable_advice.advice: Before a huge decision, try to have a 51% comfort level before committing.
actionable_advice.how_to: Like Obama, have enough information to sway the decision and don't waste time on a decision for years.

memorable_stories: Discovering half-siblings through a Love Island contestant.
The challenges and backlash from choosing to front a documentary about black maternal mortality.
The constant pressures and judgment of the public's eye as a mom to always be hot and have it together.
contrarian_claims: You do not need to be a master of everything, focus on the 'A' items and not the 'D' items.
The 'no'es you make are much more important than the 'yes'es you make in your professional and personal life.
Dysfunctional families can still teach you valuable lessons.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Rochelle Humes: Learning To Be At Peace With Uncertainty
